project.name: DocSpec Annotations (Java)
project.description: Annotation library for DocSpec universal documentation specification

methods.name: name
methods.description: Name of the input parameter

methods.name: source
methods.description: Origin or source of the input value, such as environment, request.body, or config

methods.name: description
methods.description: Prose description of the input's purpose and expected format

methods.name: items
methods.description: Enumeration of specific items, keys, or variants that this input accepts

attributes.value: Describes a single runtime input within a DocContext. This annotation has an empty Target and is designed exclusively for use as a nested annotation inside DocContext#inputs().

methods.name: artifact
methods.description: Maven-style coordinates or identifier of the referenced artifact

methods.name: what
methods.description: Name of the specific component, class, or resource being used from the artifact

methods.name: why
methods.description: Prose explanation of why this dependency exists

attributes.value: Declares a cross-project usage link within a DocContext. This annotation has an empty Target and is designed exclusively for use as a nested annotation inside DocContext#uses().

methods.name: channel
methods.description: The AsyncAPI channel name

methods.name: operation
methods.description: The operation type on this channel, such as publish or subscribe

attributes.value: Marks a class that implements an AsyncAPI specification. AsyncAPI describes event-driven architectures with channels, messages, and operations. This annotation captures the channel and operation type, enabling documentation tooling to generate AsyncAPI-compatible references and message catalogs.

methods.name: value
methods.description: The audience level for this element, must be one of public, partner, or internal

attributes.value: Declares the intended audience visibility level for the annotated element. Documentation processors use this annotation to partition API documentation into tiers. Supported values are public, partner, and internal.

methods.name: value
methods.description: A description of the architectural boundary, such as anti-corruption layer, adapter, facade, or gateway

attributes.value: Marks an architectural boundary. Boundaries are key structural points in a system where one subsystem or context meets another. Common boundary types include anti-corruption layers, adapters, facades, and gateway services. Documenting boundaries helps developers understand system decomposition and integration points.

methods.name: value
methods.description: The command name

methods.name: aggregate
methods.description: The target aggregate that this command modifies

attributes.value: Documents a command in a CQRS (Command Query Responsibility Segregation) architecture. Commands represent write operations that change state. This annotation captures the command name and the aggregate it targets, enabling documentation tooling to generate command catalogs and aggregate diagrams.

methods.name: value
methods.description: Optional description of the commutative property

attributes.value: Marks a method as commutative -- the order of its arguments does not affect the result. This annotation generates property-based tests verifying that f(a, b) == f(b, a) for all valid input pairs. Used by DSTI test generators.

methods.name: value
methods.description: A description of the comparison semantics, such as natural ordering, by-name, by-date, or case-insensitive

attributes.value: Documents comparison semantics for the annotated method. Use this annotation to describe how a method compares, orders, or evaluates equality of objects. This is particularly useful for custom comparators, equality checks, and sorting implementations.

methods.name: value
methods.description: A description of the conservation law that holds for this method

attributes.value: Declares a conservation law -- a quantity that is preserved through a transformation. Conservation laws are fundamental semantic properties stating that certain quantities remain constant before and after an operation. For example, a fund transfer must conserve the total balance across all accounts.

methods.name: id
methods.description: Unique identifier for this context

methods.name: name
methods.description: Human-readable display name for the context

methods.name: inputs
methods.description: Runtime inputs required by this context

methods.name: flow
methods.description: Identifier of the flow associated with this context

methods.name: uses
methods.description: Cross-project dependencies consumed by this context

attributes.value: Documents the runtime context for the annotated type, including its inputs, associated flow, and cross-project dependencies. A context represents the runtime environment or configuration surface that a component requires to operate. Documentation tooling can use this to generate configuration guides and dependency matrices.

methods.name: value
methods.description: Optional description of what makes this method deterministic

attributes.value: Marks a method as deterministic -- the same inputs always produce the same outputs. A deterministic method has no dependency on external state, randomness, or timing. This property enables caching, memoization, and formal verification.

methods.name: value
methods.description: The HTTP method and path of the API endpoint, formatted as METHOD /path

attributes.value: Maps the annotated SDK method to its underlying REST API endpoint. This annotation enables documentation tooling to cross-reference SDK methods with API endpoint documentation, providing consumers with a clear mapping between the programmatic interface and the HTTP contract.

methods.name: code
methods.description: Application-specific error code

methods.name: httpStatus
methods.description: HTTP status code associated with this error, -1 indicates no specific HTTP status

methods.name: description
methods.description: Human-readable description of the error condition

methods.name: causes
methods.description: Possible root causes that may lead to this error

methods.name: resolution
methods.description: Guidance for the API consumer on how to resolve or avoid this error

methods.name: since
methods.description: Version in which this error code was first introduced

attributes.value: Documents an error code on the annotated type, providing structured information for API consumers to understand and handle failures. Each annotated type represents a distinct error condition with its own code, HTTP status, description, possible causes, and resolution guidance.

methods.name: name
methods.description: Canonical name of the event, typically in dot-separated format

methods.name: description
methods.description: Human-readable description of what this event represents

methods.name: trigger
methods.description: Description of the condition or action that causes this event to fire

methods.name: channel
methods.description: The delivery channel or transport mechanism for this event, such as webhooks, sns, or kafka

methods.name: deliveryGuarantee
methods.description: Delivery guarantee semantics, such as at-most-once, at-least-once, or exactly-once

methods.name: retryPolicy
methods.description: Description of the retry policy applied when delivery fails

methods.name: since
methods.description: Version in which this event was first introduced

attributes.value: Documents a webhook or event that the annotated type or method produces or handles. This annotation captures the full contract of an event, including its delivery semantics, retry behavior, and the conditions under which it fires.

methods.name: title
methods.description: Display title for the example

methods.name: language
methods.description: Programming language of the example code, used for syntax highlighting

methods.name: code
methods.description: Inline source code for the example

methods.name: file
methods.description: Path to an external file containing the example source code

attributes.value: Attaches a code example to a documented method or type. This annotation is Repeatable, so multiple examples can be attached to the same element. Each example may supply code inline via code() or reference an external file via file().

methods.name: value
methods.description: The contained DocExample annotations

attributes.value: Container annotation for repeatable DocExample annotations. This annotation is not intended to be used directly. When multiple DocExample annotations are placed on the same element, the compiler automatically wraps them in a DocExamples container.

methods.name: description
methods.description: Prose description of the field's purpose

methods.name: since
methods.description: Version in which this field was first introduced

attributes.value: Marks a field for explicit inclusion in generated documentation. Fields that are not annotated may still appear in documentation depending on the processor configuration, but annotating a field guarantees its inclusion and allows supplying additional metadata.

methods.name: id
methods.description: Unique identifier for this flow

methods.name: name
methods.description: Human-readable display name for the flow

methods.name: description
methods.description: Prose description of what the flow accomplishes

methods.name: trigger
methods.description: Description of the event or condition that initiates this flow

methods.name: steps
methods.description: Ordered sequence of steps that compose this flow

attributes.value: Defines a multi-step documentation flow on the annotated type. A flow describes a sequence of Steps that together represent a business process, pipeline, or workflow. Documentation tooling can render flows as diagrams, step-by-step guides, or interactive walkthroughs.

methods.name: type
methods.description: The GraphQL type, such as Query, Mutation, or Subscription

methods.name: field
methods.description: The GraphQL field name

attributes.value: Marks a GraphQL resolver or type. Use this annotation to document GraphQL queries, mutations, and subscriptions. Documentation tooling can use this metadata to generate GraphQL schema references alongside the rest of the API surface.

methods.name: service
methods.description: The gRPC service name

methods.name: method
methods.description: The gRPC method name

attributes.value: Marks a gRPC service or method. Use this annotation to document gRPC service implementations and their individual RPC methods. Documentation tooling can use this metadata to generate gRPC API references alongside REST and other protocol documentation.

Marker annotation that excludes the annotated element from generated documentation. Apply this to types, methods, fields, constructors, or packages that should remain undocumented regardless of other visibility rules or processor configuration.

methods.name: value
methods.description: Optional description of the idempotency guarantee

attributes.value: Marks a method as idempotent. An idempotent method produces the same result whether it is called once or multiple times with the same arguments. This is a critical property for retry safety, distributed systems, and API design.

methods.name: value
methods.description: A description of the method's primary intent

methods.name: preserves
methods.description: Fields or properties that must be preserved (unchanged) through the method's execution

attributes.value: Declares the primary intent of the annotated method. Intent documentation captures why a method exists and what it is meant to achieve, as opposed to its mechanical implementation. This enables tooling to verify that code behavior aligns with stated intent and to detect semantic drift over time.

methods.name: on
methods.description: The entity or class the invariant applies to, empty means the annotated type itself

methods.name: rules
methods.description: The invariant rules expressed as human-readable expressions

attributes.value: Declares invariants that must hold on the annotated type. An invariant is a condition that must be true at all observable points during the lifetime of an instance. Documenting invariants enables verification tooling and helps developers understand the contracts that govern a type's state.

methods.name: value
methods.description: Description of what this method does

methods.name: returns
methods.description: Description of the return value

methods.name: params
methods.description: Structured parameter documentation

methods.name: throwsDoc
methods.description: Structured throws documentation

methods.name: since
methods.description: Version in which this method was first introduced

methods.name: deprecated
methods.description: Deprecation notice, empty means not deprecated

methods.name: example
methods.description: Code example showing usage of this method

attributes.value: Documents a method -- replaces JavaDoc comments. The value serves as the method description and is used by both DocSpec (for structured documentation) and by the docspec:inject-sources goal to generate standard JavaDoc comments for IDE hover and Maven JavaDoc.

methods.name: id
methods.description: Unique identifier for this module, used to group related types across packages

methods.name: name
methods.description: Human-readable display name for the module

methods.name: description
methods.description: Prose description of the module's purpose and scope

methods.name: since
methods.description: Version in which this module was first introduced

methods.name: audience
methods.description: Intended audience for this module's documentation, such as public, partner, or internal

attributes.value: Groups annotated types or packages into a logical documentation module. A module represents a cohesive unit of functionality within the API surface. Every type or package belonging to the same module should share the same id(), allowing documentation tooling to aggregate and present them together.

methods.name: direction
methods.description: The direction of monotonic change, valid values are increasing and decreasing

attributes.value: Declares a monotonically increasing or decreasing property. A monotonic value is one that only changes in a single direction over time. Common examples include sequence counters, timestamps, and version numbers. Documenting monotonicity helps reasoning about consistency and ordering in distributed systems.

Marker annotation that declares a method parameter as optional. Documentation processors use this annotation to distinguish required parameters from optional ones when generating API references. The actual default value semantics are determined by the implementing method.

methods.name: value
methods.description: A description of the ordering guarantee

attributes.value: Declares ordering guarantees provided by the annotated method. Use this annotation to document whether a method preserves insertion order, returns results sorted by a particular key, or provides any other ordering contract that callers may depend on.

methods.name: expectedLatency
methods.description: The expected latency for this method

methods.name: bottleneck
methods.description: A known performance bottleneck in this method

attributes.value: Documents performance characteristics of the annotated method. Use this annotation to capture expected latency targets, known bottlenecks, and other performance-related metadata. This information helps API consumers set appropriate timeouts and understand service level expectations.

methods.name: value
methods.description: The type of PII contained in this field, such as email, phone, name, address, ssn, dob, ip, financial, health, biometric, or other

methods.name: retention
methods.description: The retention policy governing how long this data may be stored

methods.name: gdprBasis
methods.description: The GDPR legal basis for processing this personal data, such as consent, contract, legal-obligation, vital-interest, public-task, or legitimate-interest

methods.name: encrypted
methods.description: Whether this field is stored in encrypted form

methods.name: neverLog
methods.description: Whether this field must never appear in log output

methods.name: neverReturn
methods.description: Whether this field must never be returned in API responses

attributes.value: Marks a field as containing Personally Identifiable Information (PII). This annotation signals to documentation tooling, logging frameworks, and data governance systems that the annotated field contains sensitive personal data requiring special handling under privacy regulations such as GDPR, CCPA, or HIPAA.

methods.name: fields
methods.description: The names of fields or properties that must remain unchanged after the method executes

attributes.value: Declares fields that must be preserved through the method's execution. A preserved field is one whose value must remain unchanged after the method completes, regardless of any intermediate mutations. This is a semantic contract that documentation and verification tooling can use to detect accidental side effects.

methods.name: value
methods.description: The type of relationship, such as parent-child, peer, depends-on, owns, or references

methods.name: target
methods.description: The target entity of this relationship

attributes.value: Documents a relationship between entities. Use this annotation to describe how a field or method relates to another entity in the system. This metadata enables documentation tooling to render entity-relationship diagrams and dependency graphs.

methods.name: reason
methods.description: The reason this data is considered sensitive

attributes.value: Marks sensitive data that should not be logged or returned in API responses. Unlike DocPII, which specifically identifies personally identifiable information, this annotation covers any data that is sensitive for security, compliance, or business reasons -- such as API keys, internal identifiers, or trade secrets.

methods.name: attachTo
methods.description: Fully qualified reference to the documented element that this example should be attached to

methods.name: title
methods.description: Display title for the example

attributes.value: Marks a test method as a verified, executable example that should be attached to a specific documented element. Unlike DocExample, which embeds code snippets as strings, this annotation designates an actual test method whose source code is extracted at build time and attached to the referenced element. Because the example is a real test, it is guaranteed to compile and pass.

methods.name: states
methods.description: The set of valid states in the state machine

methods.name: initial
methods.description: The initial state when the state machine is created

methods.name: transitions
methods.description: The allowed transitions expressed in FROM -> TO format

attributes.value: Documents a state machine on the annotated type. Use this annotation to describe the valid states, initial state, and allowed transitions for an entity or service that follows a state machine pattern. Documentation tooling can render state diagrams from this metadata.

methods.name: value
methods.description: One or more tags to associate with the annotated type

attributes.value: Associates searchable tags with a documented type. Tags provide a lightweight taxonomy that documentation tooling can use for filtering, searching, and cross-referencing related types.

methods.name: reason
methods.description: The reason why automated test generation should be skipped for this method

attributes.value: Marks a method where automated test generation should be skipped. Some methods are inherently difficult or inappropriate for automated test generation, for example methods with complex external dependencies, non-deterministic behavior, or destructive side effects. This annotation signals to test generation tooling that the method should be excluded.

methods.name: value
methods.description: The testing strategy or level, such as unit, integration, e2e, or property-based

methods.name: scenarios
methods.description: Key test scenarios that should be covered

attributes.value: Documents the test strategy for the annotated type or method. This annotation captures how a component should be tested, including the testing level and key scenarios. Documentation tooling can use this to generate test coverage reports and identify gaps.

methods.name: artifact
methods.description: Maven-style coordinates or identifier of the referenced artifact

methods.name: flow
methods.description: Identifier of the referenced flow within the artifact

methods.name: step
methods.description: Identifier of the referenced step within the flow

methods.name: member
methods.description: Name of the referenced member (method, field, class) within the artifact

methods.name: description
methods.description: Prose description of why this reference exists and how the dependency is used

attributes.value: Declares a cross-project or cross-module reference from the annotated element to an artifact, flow, step, or member in another project. This annotation is Repeatable, allowing multiple cross-project references on the same element. Documentation tooling can use these references to build dependency graphs and cross-link documentation across repositories.

methods.name: value
methods.description: The contained DocUses annotations

attributes.value: Container annotation for repeatable DocUses annotations. This annotation is not intended to be used directly. When multiple DocUses annotations are placed on the same element, the compiler automatically wraps them in a DocUsesAll container.

methods.name: path
methods.description: The WebSocket endpoint path

methods.name: messages
methods.description: The message types handled by this WebSocket endpoint

attributes.value: Marks a WebSocket endpoint. Use this annotation to document WebSocket endpoints and the message types they handle. Documentation tooling can use this metadata to generate WebSocket API references.

methods.name: name
methods.description: Parameter name -- must match the actual Java parameter name

methods.name: value
methods.description: Description of the parameter

attributes.value: Documents a method parameter. Used inside DocMethod#params(). Replaces the @param tag in JavaDoc comments.

methods.name: id
methods.description: Unique identifier for this step within its flow

methods.name: name
methods.description: Human-readable display name for the step

methods.name: actor
methods.description: Short name of the actor (system, service, or role) responsible for executing this step

methods.name: actorQualified
methods.description: Fully qualified name of the actor, such as a class or service identifier

methods.name: description
methods.description: Prose description of what this step does

methods.name: type
methods.description: The type of this step, indicating its role in the flow

methods.name: ai
methods.description: Whether this step involves an AI or LLM invocation

methods.name: retryTarget
methods.description: Identifier of the step to retry or fall back to on failure

methods.name: inputs
methods.description: Names or identifiers of the data inputs consumed by this step

methods.name: outputs
methods.description: Names or identifiers of the data outputs produced by this step

attributes.value: Describes a single step within a DocFlow. This annotation has an empty Target and is designed exclusively for use as a nested annotation inside DocFlow#steps(). Supported type values are process, ai, storage, trigger, retry, external, bridge, and observability.

methods.name: type
methods.description: Fully qualified or simple name of the exception type

methods.name: value
methods.description: Description of when this exception is thrown

attributes.value: Documents a thrown exception. Used inside DocMethod#throwsDoc(). Replaces the @throws tag in JavaDoc comments.
